mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession probability spikes, causing institutional investors to de-risk portfolios and driving negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with decelerating inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering monetary tightening, producing positive quarterly equity returns through earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During active rate hiking periods, an accelerating industrial production index signals resilient corporate output that offsets higher discount rates, yielding positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of tightening-cycle observations, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) across multiple hiking episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP reflects growing corporate revenue fundamentals and economic momentum, which mechanically supports higher equity prices and generates positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in over 50 percent of expansionary GDP periods, or the correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns falls below 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 320, persistent inflation forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of threshold-crossing events, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during subsequent inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, forecasting negative quarterly market performance.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of late-cycle housing decline periods, or the signal loses predictive power when housing supply constraints dominate demand shocks.
